_sources.discrepancies: NCSL lists LA as 'photo ID requested' (non-strict). SoS confirms voters without photo ID may sign an affidavit and cast a regular ballot. Louisiana uses a jungle primary (top-two) system, not a party primary runoff. The statewide primary is a nonpartisan jungle primary; runoffs exist but are not party-locked. No party-locked runoff rule applies.

earlyVoting.notes: Early voting runs 14 to 7 days before the election (excluding Sunday). TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting for general/runoff. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting runs from 14 to 7 days before the election (Sundays excluded). Hours set by parish. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

acceptedIds: Louisiana driver's license or ID card
Louisiana special ID card (free for qualifying voters)
Student ID from an accredited Louisiana school
Hunting or fishing license issued by Louisiana
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID must not be expired. Louisiana issues free photo IDs for voters who cannot obtain one. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: Voters without photo ID may cast a provisional ballot. Alternatively, a poll commissioner who knows the voter may vouch for their identity.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Louisiana prohibits use of wireless communication devices in the voting booth. You may bring written notes.
additionalRules: Louisiana uses a jungle primary (nonpartisan blanket primary): all candidates appear on one ballot regardless of party. If no candidate receives a majority, the top two advance to a general election runoff.
Party-locked runoff rules do not apply — Louisiana's runoff is between the top-two finishers regardless of party.

eligibility: Will be out of the parish on Election Day and early voting period
Caretaker of ill or disabled person
Employed as an election commissioner or official
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Must be received by the registrar of voters 4 days before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the registrar of voters by 4:30 p.m. on the day before the election, or by 8 p.m. on Election Day if hand-delivered
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Absentee ballot must arrive at the registrar by 4:30 p.m. the day before Election Day (mail), or by 8 p.m. Election Day (in person).

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Louisiana uses a jungle primary (non-partisan blanket primary). All candidates appear on a single ballot regardless of party. A candidate who wins more than 50% wins outright; otherwise the top two advance to a general election runoff.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Luisiana utiliza una primaria abierta (primaria de boleta única no partidista). Todos los candidatos aparecen en una sola boleta sin importar el partido. Un candidato que obtenga más del 50% gana directamente; de lo contrario, los dos primeros avanzan a una segunda vuelta en las elecciones generales.
